skill.name: gws-meet
skill.description: Manage Google Meet conference records, spaces, participants, recordings, smart notes, and transcripts through the gws CLI.
skill.summary: Operate Google Meet API resources from an AI assistant using the gws command-line tool.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography and Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown text and inline code formatting. The skill does instruct agents to run the gws CLI against Google Meet resources, including operations that can create, update, or end meeting spaces, so publication should include a permission and command-review warning.

medium_findings.title: External Google Workspace CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to run gws commands. This is expected for the skill, but these commands can act with the authenticated user's Google Workspace permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain explicit gws command examples. The commands are legitimate for this skill, but they create real external side effects when used with authenticated credentials.

medium_findings.title: Meeting Space Modification Capabilities
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The documented Meet operations include creating, ending, retrieving, and patching meeting spaces. These actions can change live Google Meet resources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The methods are listed directly in the skill content. The finding is medium severity because the actions are intended, but they require user confirmation and proper account permissions.

low_findings.title: Relative Shared Skill Reference
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for path traversal: The relative path points to a sibling shared skill file used for authentication and security guidance. It does not contain dynamic file access or user-controlled traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line references a fixed relative Markdown path and a fixed gws generation command. No evidence found of untrusted path input or filesystem exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Only
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for suspicious network behavior: The hardcoded URLs are Google developer documentation links for Meet API examples. They are not data submission endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to developers.google.com documentation pages. No evidence found of outbound credential transfer or hidden callback endpoints.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary words or Markdown content. The skill contains no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Meet functionality and documentation links. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic API usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: State-Changing API Command Surface
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill exposes commands that can create, patch, and end Google Meet spaces. This is not malicious, but users should review generated commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The state-changing methods are named in the documented API resource list. Impact depends on the user's authenticated Google Workspace permissions.

content.value_statement: Google Meet records and spaces can be hard to inspect from memory during support and operations work. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use the gws CLI for focused Meet API tasks.
actual_capabilities: Lists available Google Meet conference record resources and methods.
Guides inspection of gws meet commands before calling an API method.
Supports conference records, participants, recordings, smart notes, and transcripts.
Supports meeting space create, get, patch, and end active conference operations.
Directs agents to use gws schema output for parameters and JSON flags.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI to be installed and authenticated before use.
Depends on Google Workspace account permissions for each Meet API operation.
Does not provide standalone authentication setup inside this skill file.
Can change meeting spaces, so generated commands require user review.

use_cases.title: Review Meeting History
use_cases.description: List recent conference records and inspect details for a support or compliance request.

use_cases.title: Manage Meeting Spaces
use_cases.description: Create, retrieve, update, or end a Meet space using the authenticated gws CLI.

use_cases.title: Collect Meeting Artifacts
use_cases.description: Find recordings, transcripts, participants, or smart notes tied to a conference record.

prompt_templates.title: Show Meet Commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws-meet skill to show me the available Google Meet resources and methods. Do not run a state-changing command.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Conference Records
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gws schema to inspect the conferenceRecords list method, then prepare the safest command to list recent Meet conference records.

prompt_templates.title: Find Meeting Artifacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me inspect the schema for Meet recordings and transcripts under a conference record, then draft commands to retrieve the relevant artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Update A Meeting Space
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the schema for spaces.patch, explain the required parameters, and prepare a command for review before changing the meeting space.

output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the conferenceRecords list method, checks the schema, and presents a read-only gws command for user approval.

output_examples.input: End the active conference for this meeting space.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies the spaces.endActiveConference method, explains the effect, and asks for confirmation before preparing the command.

output_examples.input: Find transcripts for a completed meeting.
output_examples.output: The assistant maps the request to the transcripts resource and prepares the needed conference record lookup steps.

best_practices: Inspect each method with gws schema before building parameters.
Use read-only commands first when investigating conference records.
Review every create, patch, or end command before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not run state-changing Meet commands without explicit user approval.
Do not assume the authenticated account has the correct Workspace permissions.
Do not paste meeting transcripts or recordings into unrelated conversations.

faq.question: What does this skill manage?
faq.answer: It manages Google Meet conference records, meeting spaces, participants, recordings, smart notes, and transcripts through the gws CLI.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The gws CLI must be installed and authenticated with an account that has the needed Google Workspace permissions.

faq.question: Can it change live meetings?
faq.answer: Yes. The spaces methods include create, patch, and end active conference operations, so review commands before execution.

faq.question: Does it include API examples?
faq.answer: It links to Google developer documentation for meeting space examples and directs agents to inspect schemas locally.

faq.question: Is it safe for read-only audits?
faq.answer: It can support read-only audits when limited to list and get operations. Avoid patch, create, or end methods during audits.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
